content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to accept the following primitive data types from user:


int
float
char

Input Format
The only line of input contains an integer, a floating point value and a character value, all separated by a space.



Output Format

Print the integer, float and char values respectively.
Print the floating point value rounded upto 3 decimal places.
constraints: character is any printable ASCII character
content.inputFormat: The only line contains an integer, a floating‑point number and a printable ASCII character separated by single spaces.
content.outputFormat: Print the integer, the floating‑point number rounded to exactly three decimal places, and the character, separated by single spaces.

solution.approach: Read the three values using standard input. The integer and character are printed unchanged. The floating‑point value is printed with exactly three digits after the decimal point using the appropriate format specifier ("%.3f" in C, "fixed<<setprecision(3)" in C++, "System.out.printf("%d %.3f %c", ...)" in Java, f"{value:.3f}" in Python, and toFixed(3) in JavaScript). The algorithm runs in O(1) time and uses O(1) extra space.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if len(data) < 3:
        return
    a = int(data[0])
    b = float(data[1])
    c = data[2]
    sys.stdout.write(f"{a} {b:.3f} {c}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
